phases.label: Level 1
phases.why: Your mantra poke-and-speed denial out-muscle Anivia's wall-frost poke control at level 1 — this window is yours, so force the trades and tax every step toward the wave.

phases.label: Level 2
phases.why: At level 2 your mantra poke-and-speed denial beat the wall-frost poke control in a straight exchange. Play the front foot — make Anivia answer you, not the other way around.

phases.label: Level 3
phases.why: Your mantra poke-and-speed denial out-muscle Anivia's wall-frost poke control at level 3 — this window is yours, so force the trades and tax every step toward the wave.

phases.label: Levels 4-5
phases.why: Through levels 4-5 your mantra poke-and-speed denial beat the wall-frost poke control in a straight exchange. Play the front foot — make Anivia answer you, not the other way around.

phases.label: Level 6
phases.why: Slight deficit at the level-6 breakpoint: Anivia's wall-frost poke control edge out your mantra poke-and-speed denial on the margins. You can still trade — just let him start the exchanges and answer on your terms.

phases.label: First item
phases.why: On first item the numbers lean his way — the wall-frost poke control out-tick your mantra poke-and-speed denial in even exchanges. Don't bleed for pride; take the trades his cooldowns hand you.

phases.label: 2+ items
phases.why: Late Karma is a utility engine: RE shields the team, W roots the diver, and RQ pokes the objective setup.

phases.label: Level 1
phases.why: Q-poke wins level 1 against nearly everyone — the lane starts as your spam war; respect only mana.

phases.label: Level 2
phases.why: Mantra online from level 1 means RQ is already a third of a health bar — spend it on real windows, not waves.

phases.label: Level 3
phases.why: Full kit: W tether roots overstayers and E speeds every trade exit. Your early is the best in the utility class.

phases.label: Levels 4-5
phases.why: Your mantra poke-and-speed denial out-muscle Aurelion Sol's stardust ramp control through levels 4-5 — this window is yours, so force the trades and tax every step toward the wave.

phases.label: Level 6
phases.why: At the level-6 breakpoint the numbers lean your way — mantra poke-and-speed denial edge out the stardust ramp control when both sides play it straight. Press the small advantages: wave position, first poke, cooldown ticks.

phases.label: First item
phases.why: Blackfire/Luden's spike: RQ-W chains threaten kills outright. The lane is now or never — you fall off politely.

phases.label: 2+ items
phases.why: Late Karma is a utility engine: RE shields the team, W roots the diver, and RQ pokes the objective setup.

phases.label: Level 1
phases.why: At level 1 your mantra poke-and-speed denial beat the grounded-zone DPS in a straight exchange. Play the front foot — make Cassiopeia answer you, not the other way around.

phases.label: Level 2
phases.why: Slight edge at level 2: your mantra poke-and-speed denial beat Cassiopeia's grounded-zone DPS on the margins. Take the short trades you start — just don't force a coin-flip all-in off a small lead.

phases.label: Level 3
phases.why: Full kit: W tether roots overstayers and E speeds every trade exit. Your early is the best in the utility class.

phases.label: Levels 4-5
phases.why: Slight deficit through levels 4-5: Cassiopeia's grounded-zone DPS edge out your mantra poke-and-speed denial on the margins. You can still trade — just let him start the exchanges and answer on your terms.

phases.label: Level 6
phases.why: Respect window: at the level-6 breakpoint the grounded-zone DPS beat your mantra poke-and-speed denial cleanly. Trade only off Cassiopeia's cooldowns and keep the wave where the lead can't cash out.

phases.label: First item
phases.why: Cassiopeia's grounded-zone DPS own this window — your mantra poke-and-speed denial don't answer them on first item yet. Farm what's safe and let the next spike re-price the lane.

phases.why: Lost Chapter timing — the RQ chunk cycles every 30 seconds. Chip him below half and hold the kill window.

phases.label: Level 6
phases.why: No new ult — your 6 is a Mantra rank: faster cycles, bigger RQs. Press the early lead before scaling lanes wake up.

phases.label: First item
phases.why: Zed's shadow-shuriken executes own this window — your mantra poke-and-speed denial don't answer them on first item yet. Farm what's safe and let the next spike re-price the lane.

phases.label: 2+ items
phases.why: Late Karma is a utility engine: RE shields the team, W roots the diver, and RQ pokes the objective setup.

phases.label: Level 1
phases.why: Your mantra poke-and-speed denial out-muscle Galio's anti-magic shield trades at level 1 — this window is yours, so force the trades and tax every step toward the wave.

phases.label: Level 2
phases.why: At level 2 your mantra poke-and-speed denial beat the anti-magic shield trades in a straight exchange. Play the front foot — make Galio answer you, not the other way around.

phases.label: Level 3
phases.why: Slight edge at level 3: your mantra poke-and-speed denial beat Galio's anti-magic shield trades on the margins. Take the short trades you start — just don't force a coin-flip all-in off a small lead.

phases.label: Levels 4-5
phases.why: Through levels 4-5 the numbers lean your way — mantra poke-and-speed denial edge out the anti-magic shield trades when both sides play it straight. Press the small advantages: wave position, first poke, cooldown ticks.

phases.label: Level 6
phases.why: Slight deficit at the level-6 breakpoint: Galio's anti-magic shield trades edge out your mantra poke-and-speed denial on the margins. You can still trade — just let him start the exchanges and answer on your terms.

phases.label: First item
phases.why: Blackfire/Luden's spike: RQ-W chains threaten kills outright. The lane is now or never — you fall off politely.

phases.label: 2+ items
phases.why: Slight deficit at two-plus items: Galio's anti-magic shield trades edge out your mantra poke-and-speed denial on the margins. You can still trade — just let him start the exchanges and answer on your terms.

phases.why: Respect window: on first item the wave-dash blade stacks beat your mantra poke-and-speed denial cleanly. Trade only off Irelia's cooldowns and keep the wave where the lead can't cash out.
